title: The piano in the corner
summary: A fun fact about how Piano Man almost was not recorded at all.
body:
Billy Joel's record label was not enthusiastic about Piano Man when he pitched it. It was too long, too slow, too much like a story. They wanted something tighter.

He recorded it anyway. It became the song that defined his career.

There is a lesson in there somewhere about trusting the long, slow things even when someone tells you to cut them short.
